sections.heading: New York Mets vs St. Louis Cardinals
sections.body: March 12, 2026 · Roger Dean Chevrolet Stadium · 01:05 PM ET

sections.heading: 1. Short Recap
sections.body: The New York Mets open their 2026 season on the road against the St. Louis Cardinals. Both teams start with fresh records. The Mets will look to capitalize on their solid 2025 offensive metrics. No recent injuries or lineup changes have been reported.

sections.heading: 2. Pitching Matchup
sections.body: Sean Manaea starts for the Mets after posting a 5.64 ERA last season. His 2025 advanced metrics suggest room for improvement, with a FIP of 3.94. Michael McGreevy counters for St. Louis, carrying a 4.42 ERA from last year. His recent form shows a slightly better last-three ERA of 4.28. | Pitcher | Team | W–L | ERA | WHIP | IP note | K | BB | HR/9 | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Sean Manaea | New York Mets | 2-4 | 5.64 | 1.22 | 2025 — 60.2 IP, 75 K, 12 BB | N/A | N/A | 1.93 | | Michael McGreevy | St. Louis Cardinals | 8-4 | 4.42 | 1.25 | 2025 — 95.2 IP, 58 K, 20 BB | N/A | N/A | 1.13 | | Pitcher | FIP | xERA | K% | BB% | K/BB | Hard-Hit% | Barrel% | Last 3 ERA | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Sean Manaea | 3.94 | 4.02 | 28.5% | 4.6% | 3.50 | 42.6% | 8.9% | 5.68 | | Michael McGreevy | 4.27 | 4.67 | 14.5% | 5.0% | 1.38 | 40.1% | 8.8% | 4.28 |

sections.heading: 3. Lineup Comparison
sections.body: No notable absences in the Mets lineup. Francisco Alvarez remains a power threat with 19 home runs in 2025. sections.heading: 4. Bullpen
sections.body: The Mets bullpen enters with a solid 3.71 ERA from last season. No recent workload concerns reported. The Cardinals' bullpen lacks recent performance data. | Team | ERA | xFIP | Last 14d ERA | Last 3d IP | Rating | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | New York Mets | 3.71 | 3.88 | N/A | N/A | 70/100 | | St. Louis Cardinals | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A | 65/100 |

sections.heading: 5. Key Edges
sections.body: - Sean Manaea's 2025 FIP of 3.94 indicates potential overperformance compared to his ERA. - Mets offense has a higher wRC+ (104) compared to Cardinals (96). - Mets bullpen rated higher (70/100) than Cardinals (65/100). - Mets show superior OPS (.748) over Cardinals (.718). - Manaea's K% of 28.5% suggests a strikeout advantage over McGreevy's 14.5%.

sections.heading: 6. Today's Pick
sections.body: The Mets hold several analytical edges, including a stronger bullpen and superior offensive metrics. Manaea's underlying stats suggest he could outperform his previous ERA. New York's lineup depth and bullpen advantage should secure a road win.

sections.heading: Today's Pick: New York Mets Moneyline

writeup.pickSummary: The Mets hold several analytical edges, including a stronger bullpen and superior offensive metrics. Manaea's underlying stats suggest he could outperform his previous ERA. New York's lineup depth and bullpen advantage should secure a road win.
